title: Ideal Gas Law P-V-T Compressibility Validator
description: Validates PV = nRT for a fixed air sample by stepping a motorized syringe pump through 6 volume positions (20–50 mL in 6 mL steps) at two temperatures (25 °C and 50 °C) while reading headspace pressure with a BMP280. The Arduino computes the compressibility factor Z = PV/nRT at each point and logs deviations from ideal behaviour. An over-pressure fault trips at 200 kPa.
objective: Measure compressibility factor Z at 12 P-V-T operating points and verify Z within ±2 % of unity for air at near-ambient conditions.
constraints: Arduino Uno, BMP280, DS18B20, NEMA17 stepper, A4988 driver, 20 mL syringe; max pressure 200 kPa gauge; max temperature 55 °C; no reactive gases.
supporting_fact_titles: Gibbs Free Energy and Chemical Spontaneity

element_usage_notes.BMP280 Barometric Pressure Sensor Module: Reads headspace pressure after each volume step; sampled after 30 s equilibration.
element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Monitors gas temperature to compute T_K for Z calculation; heater maintains temperature setpoints.
element_usage_notes.Motorized Syringe Pump: Steps through six volume positions; each step displaces ~6 mL and waits for pressure equilibration.
element_usage_notes.Resistance Heater with PWM Control: Warms vessel to second temperature set-point (50 °C) for the second sweep series.
element_usage_notes.SD Card Data Logger Module: Logs V_cm3, P_Pa, T_K, Z at every measurement point.

element_interactions.description: Volume step causes pressure change; BMP280 reads new equilibrium pressure
Bang-bang heater holds vessel at target temperature during each sweep
Pressure logged with corresponding volume and temperature
Gas temperature logged for compressibility factor computation
